explanation: The Sigma rule specifically filters for processes where the Image path ends with '\gpresult.exe'. By copying the binary to a different location and renaming it, the Image path requirement is no longer met, even though the functionality remains the same.

The rule triggers on the use of specific flags '/z' (super-verbose) or '/v' (verbose). Using the '/h' (HTML report) or '/r' (Summary report) flags still allows an attacker to discover Group Policy information while avoiding the specific command line arguments defined in the detection selection.

Instead of using the gpresult.exe utility, an attacker can use native PowerShell cmdlets like Get-GPOReport or Get-GPResultantSetOfPolicy to gather the same Resultant Set of Policy (RSoP) information, completely bypassing a rule focused solely on the gpresult.exe executable.
